name: React - Optimization
scope: A person with INTERMEDIATE proficiency level in ReactJs - Optimization should be able to explain React’s rendering lifecycle (render vs commit), identify what causes re-renders (props, state, context), and understand reconciliation, key usage, batching, and the difference between initial render and re-render costs. They should structure state (local, derived, global via Context, Redux, Zustand, etc.) to minimize unnecessary updates, use memoization tools (`React.memo`, `useMemo`, `useCallback`), and optimize component trees to control prop drilling and update scopes. They can apply virtualization (react-window, react-virtualized) for large lists/tables, use stable keys, and optimize list item components for performance. They understand and use data fetching libraries (React Query, SWR, Apollo) efficiently, manage effect dependencies to avoid redundant requests, and handle loading/error states with minimal layout shift. They can use React 18 features like concurrent rendering, transitions (`startTransition`, `useTransition`), Suspense, and code-splitting (`React.lazy`), structuring boundaries for good UX. They profile performance using React DevTools Profiler, browser tools (Lighthouse, Chrome Performance), and basic metrics (TTI, FID, CLS, FPS), and can design experiments to isolate bottlenecks. They understand how React updates map to DOM, layout, and paint, avoid layout thrashing, optimize images and event listeners, and consider device constraints. They organize code to localize state, use container/presenter patterns, apply code-splitting and route-based loading, and evaluate third-party libraries for performance. They write or maintain tests to guard performance-sensitive flows, verify correctness after optimizations, and collaborate with QA. They leverage build tools (Webpack, Vite, Parcel), bundle analyzers, tree-shaking, and caching strategies, and understand SPA/SSR/SSG trade-offs. They participate in code reviews for performance, document optimizations, mentor peers, and communicate trade-offs and guidelines for React performance across the team.
